title: Fix the Binary Search
description:
The following Python code claims to find if a target value exists in a sorted array, but it has a bug. Find and fix it.

```python
def binary_search(arr, target):
    lo, hi = 0, len(arr)
    while lo < hi:
        mid = (lo + hi) // 2
        if arr[mid] == target:
            return True
        elif arr[mid] < target:
            lo = mid
        else:
            hi = mid
    return False

n, t = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
print('YES' if binary_search(arr, t) else 'NO')
```

The bug causes an infinite loop for some inputs. Fix it.
inputSpec: First line: n (size) and t (target). Second line: n sorted integers.
outputSpec: YES if target found, NO otherwise.
constraints: 1 ≤ n ≤ 1000, values in [-10000, 10000], array is sorted ascending

botCodes.code:
let () =
  let n = Scanf.scanf " %d" Fun.id and t = Scanf.scanf " %d" Fun.id in
  let arr = Array.init n (fun _ -> Scanf.scanf " %d" Fun.id) in
  let lo = ref 0 and hi = ref (n-1) and found = ref false in
  while !lo <= !hi && not !found do
    let mid = (!lo + !hi) / 2 in
    if arr.(mid) = t then found := true
    else if arr.(mid) < t then lo := mid + 1
    else hi := mid - 1
  done;
  print_string (if !found then "YES" else "NO");
  print_newline ()

n, t = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
print('YES' if t in arr else 'NO')
